source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
Grey and white pearls are threaded on a piece of string.
<image1>
Monika wants to have 5 grey pearls. However, she can only pull off pearls from the end of the string. Therefore she has to pull off some white pearls as well. What is the minimum number of white pearls she has to pull off, to get 5 grey pearls?

image_dependency: question asks about pearls on a string but does not provide the pearl order in text, so the image is needed to know the sequence from the end
asset registry shows an existing primary image and registry_passed=true
multi_step: solving requires reading the pearl sequence from the end of the string
must continue removing pearls until 5 grey pearls have been pulled off
must separately count how many removed pearls are white and minimize that count under the end-removal constraint
the reasoning is more than direct lookup but still a short deterministic counting process
verifiability: given the image sequence, the result can be checked by counting removed pearls from one end until 5 grey pearls are included
single-image counting task appears to have a unique, rule-checkable outcome

decision_reasons: question, image, and numeric answer are all present, so the core semantics are well supported
image dependency is high, but the required primary image exists and registry passed
verifiability is strong because the answer is a checkable numeric counting result
no asset registry issues are reported, and the sample appears recoverable and usable without major gaps
